# Use of AI-based tools {-}

In line with the course guidelines, I used AI-based tools in a transparent and limited way:

-   GitHub Copilot (RStudio integration) was used as an assistive coding tool. It suggested snippets of R code. I critically checked each suggestion, adapted it where necessary and only kept code that I fully understood and considered appropriate for the assignment.
-   DeepL was used as a language-editing (writing-assistance) tool to improve clarity, coherence, and linguistic fluency. No substantive content was generated by DeepL.
-   ChatGPT was used to review and validate parts of my work, for example by checking coherence of argumentation, plausibility of model specifications, and clarity of explanations. I treated the feedback as suggestions, not as authoritative answers, and I only adopted elements that I critically assessed and fully understood.
- Gemini was used to refine the structural quality and documentation of the R code. Specifically, code segments were refactored to adhere to the tidyverse style guide (e.g. snake_case naming conventions). Furthermore, roxygen2-compliant documentation for functions was generated.
